metadata.executor_model: Codex configured default (thread API did not expose model id)

expectations.text: Explains why a fix that passes 780px can fail at 812px and requires tests on both sides of breakpoints.
expectations.evidence: Identifies the 781–850px risk interval where desktop labels reappear and tests 780/781/812/844/850/851.

expectations.text: Moves or protects restart with confirmation instead of only restyling it.
expectations.evidence: Moves restart into settings and specifies cancel-first confirmation, Escape cancel, and one-shot initialization.

expectations.text: Requires touch targets around 44x44 CSS pixels and preserves HP, currency, and run progress.
expectations.evidence: Requires 44×44 controls and explicitly says HP、金币和路线进度 cannot all be hidden.

expectations.text: Provides at least five concrete viewport or boundary checks, including low-height landscape.
Separates observable symptom, root cause, fix, and regression pass condition.
expectations.evidence: Response has dedicated diagnosis, root-cause comparison, minimal fix and two acceptance sections.

expectations.text: Defines a complete map-to-battle-to-reward-to-boss vertical slice with explicit exclusions.
expectations.evidence: Defines route → battle → reward → later battle → boss → result and a dedicated 首版明确不做 section.

expectations.text: Invents a desert/mechanical second construction axis that changes decisions rather than only adding stats.
expectations.evidence: The pulse-ring pointer and placed city modules make card order and pulse value decide which module activates.

expectations.text: Separates profile state from run state and addresses save schema migration.
expectations.evidence: Defines profile/run/pending/ui/preferences and requires schemaVersion plus defaults/migration for missing ring, links, or reward arrays.

expectations.text: Includes click/target/cancel interaction contracts and multi-target behavior.
expectations.evidence: Defines enemy/self targeting, invalid target, repeat-card/blank/Escape cancellation and includes a dual-enemy encounter.

expectations.text: Includes mobile height and breakpoint tests, public deployment verification, and cache busting.
expectations.evidence: Tests 1366×768, 844×390, 780×430, 568×320 and breakpoint ±1px; verifies target commit/version/resource tags and cache identity.

expectations.text: Avoids nautical, rust, tide, and copied Slay the Spire expression.
expectations.evidence: Uses an original desert walking-city pulse-ring fantasy and explicitly prohibits copied expression.

expectations.text: Defines a complete map-to-battle-to-reward-to-boss vertical slice with explicit exclusions.
expectations.evidence: Defines title/map/two normal battles/rewards/workshop/boss/result and a constrained v1 scope.

expectations.text: Invents a desert/mechanical second construction axis that changes decisions rather than only adding stats.
expectations.evidence: Six-slot city pulse track changes card order, placement, direction and boss counterplay.

expectations.text: Separates profile state from run state and addresses save schema migration.
expectations.evidence: Separates profile/run/pending and requires versioned migration for pulse direction, module turn state and route edges.

expectations.text: Includes click/target/cancel interaction contracts and multi-target behavior.
expectations.evidence: Defines enemy/self targets, invalid targets, repeat/blank/Escape cancellation, defeated targets and a two-enemy validation battle.

expectations.text: Includes mobile height and breakpoint tests, public deployment verification, and cache busting.
expectations.evidence: Includes 844×390, 780×430, 568×320, breakpoint ±1px, commit verification and CSS/JS version tags.

expectations.text: Avoids nautical, rust, tide, and copied Slay the Spire expression.
expectations.evidence: Keeps the setting, systems and expression desert/mechanical and explicitly excludes copied card frames, characters and maps.

expectations.text: Returns exactly ten non-duplicate issues spanning at least seven different quality domains.
expectations.evidence: Exactly ten numbered findings cover onboarding, targeting, responsive layout, migration, route graph, content/story variation, reward information, audio, permanent equipment, and deployment/cache.

expectations.text: Each issue contains reproduction/evidence, player impact, root cause, concrete fix, and pass condition.
expectations.evidence: Every numbered finding uses the same explicit fields: 复现、症状、玩家影响、根因、修复、通过条件、旧玩家保护.

expectations.text: Includes at least one save migration issue and one public cache/deployment issue.
expectations.evidence: Finding 4 specifies versioned migration and backup; finding 10 specifies mixed-version cache, deployed commit, build IDs, and stable URL.

expectations.text: Includes at least one strategic-truth issue such as fake routes or weak reward choice, not only UI polish.
expectations.evidence: Finding 5 tests whether route edges truly constrain clicks; finding 7 tests whether rewards support real card/equipment construction.

expectations.text: Explains compatibility protections for active players and stable links.
expectations.evidence: Every issue has an old-player protection; deployment keeps the original URL and migration namespace while adding versioned assets and rollback.

expectations.text: Does not treat HTTP 200 or syntax success as proof of visual/gameplay correctness.
expectations.evidence: Opening rejects link availability as completion; finding 10 explicitly requires build identity and mixed-cache checks beyond HTTP 200.

notes: Hypothesis-driven because source and network inspection were intentionally prohibited.

expectations.text: Explains why a fix that passes 780px can fail at 812px and requires tests on both sides of breakpoints.
expectations.evidence: Identifies a min-width breakpoint switching mobile to desktop/tablet positioning and tests 799/800/811/812/813.

expectations.text: Moves or protects restart with confirmation instead of only restyling it.
expectations.evidence: Suggests moving restart into settings and requires a cancelable confirmation before mutation.

expectations.text: Requires touch targets around 44x44 CSS pixels and preserves HP, currency, and run progress.
expectations.evidence: Requires 44×44 buttons, but never requires the compact HUD to keep HP, currency, and route progress visible.

expectations.text: Provides at least five concrete viewport or boundary checks, including low-height landscape.
expectations.evidence: Lists nine boundary widths and explicitly checks low-height phone landscape and safe areas.

expectations.text: Separates observable symptom, root cause, fix, and regression pass condition.
expectations.evidence: Provides diagnosis, constrained layout repair, restart repair, and separate acceptance tests.

expectations.text: Defines a complete map-to-battle-to-reward-to-boss vertical slice with explicit exclusions.
expectations.evidence: Defines a complete route/combat/reward/boss run and a dedicated list of excluded v1 features.

expectations.text: Invents a desert/mechanical second construction axis that changes decisions rather than only adding stats.
expectations.evidence: The instruction deck × wheel-chain system makes card order and stored pulse timing matter.

expectations.text: Separates profile state from run state and addresses save schema migration.
expectations.evidence: Separates profile and run and stores schemaVersion, but only says incompatible saves should show a warning; no concrete migration path or defaults are provided.

expectations.text: Includes click/target/cancel interaction contracts and multi-target behavior.
expectations.evidence: Includes click-card/click-target and Escape cancellation, but does not specify multi-target selection behavior or a multi-enemy target validation case.

expectations.text: Includes mobile height and breakpoint tests, public deployment verification, and cache busting.
expectations.evidence: Has a useful viewport list and HTTPS public check, but lacks breakpoint ±1px coverage, deployed-commit verification and cache-busting validation.

expectations.text: Avoids nautical, rust, tide, and copied Slay the Spire expression.
expectations.evidence: Uses original desert mechanical-city terms and explicitly avoids copied expression.

expectations.text: Returns exactly ten non-duplicate issues spanning at least seven different quality domains.
expectations.evidence: Exactly ten findings span save, map input, combat, reward transaction, equipment, story, storage integrity, audio, mobile, and deployment.

expectations.text: Each issue contains reproduction/evidence, player impact, root cause, concrete fix, and pass condition.
expectations.evidence: All findings consistently include reproduction, impact, root cause, repair, pass condition, and old-player protection.

expectations.text: Includes at least one save migration issue and one public cache/deployment issue.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 covers historical save migration; finding 10 covers mixed-version static resources and cache.

expectations.text: Includes at least one strategic-truth issue such as fake routes or weak reward choice, not only UI polish.
expectations.evidence: Map and reward findings focus on double-click/idempotency failures. The response never tests whether route branches constrain strategy or whether reward candidates create meaningful construction choices.

expectations.text: Explains compatibility protections for active players and stable links.
expectations.evidence: It gives extensive old-save/static-resource protection, but does not require preserving a canonical direct-play URL or distinguish the stable play link from repository/deployment destinations.

expectations.text: Does not treat HTTP 200 or syntax success as proof of visual/gameplay correctness.
expectations.evidence: The introduction explicitly states that link accessibility does not prove functional correctness and the checks remain behavior-driven.

notes: Hypothesis-driven because source and network inspection were intentionally prohibited.
Eval 1 is weakly discriminating: the prompt itself supplies breakpoint and restart clues, so baseline still reaches 80%.

Eval 2 is the strongest discriminator: both with-skill repeats pass all six expectations; baseline misses concrete migration, multi-target validation, breakpoint-adjacent checks, deployed-commit verification, and cache busting.
The two with-skill analog repeats both score 100% (min=max=100%, standard deviation 0), but differ substantially in length and execution time.

All three initial eval-2 turns completed without a final response and required an explicit follow-up. This is an execution-reliability failure shared by skill and baseline, not hidden by the content pass rate.
Eval 3 fair baseline is strong on transaction safety but misses strategic route/reward truth and canonical stable-link protection; with-skill covers both.
The first eval-3 pair was invalidated because only the with-skill run inspected source and live endpoints; it is excluded from this benchmark.
Token counts were unavailable. The benchmark token column is output character count used only as a rough size proxy.
With-skill time is higher overall, but run counts are asymmetric because eval 2 includes a second with-skill repeat for variance measurement.
